mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which fuels short-term equity momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, with Rank IC dropping below 0.01 during high-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation above 0.4% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >65% of high-inflation months or inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows or GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and manufacturing slowdown, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative threshold events yield positive forward returns in >60% of cases or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
